hunch · engineering memory

<!-- hunch:topic sign-in dec_a41b0c37e2 -->

status: current topic: sign-in

Why this door is the way it is.

Your graph remembers the arguments your team already settled — decisions, bug lineage, the constraints that must not break. Signing in scopes that memory to you: your private overlay, your provenance, your corrections.

rejected alternatives

  • ✗magic links — an email roundtrip loses to a password manager every time
  • ✗SSO-only — solo maintainers with a private overlay come first

provenance: commit 7cca005 · confidence 0.96

Sign in

Pick up where your graph left off.

Forgot password?

No account yet? Hunch runs in your repo — start with npx hunch init, then ask your maintainer for an overlay invite.
